channel.title: Social Media on Lucky Taorem | Tech & AI Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Social Media on Lucky Taorem | Tech & AI Blog

item.title: X Algorithm Update Prioritizes Replies
item.description:
Introduction to X’s Algorithm Update  

X, a popular social media platform, has recently announced an update to its algorithm. The update aims to prioritize replies from people you already follow, making the reply section feel less like a ‘battleground’ and more like a space for meaningful conversations. This change is intended to improve the user experience and encourage more engaging discussions.

The Problem with the Current Algorithm  

According to Nikita Bier, X’s head of product, the current algorithm was resulting in the reply section feeling more like a battleground with people you don’t recognize. This was due to the algorithm not taking into account the fact that users were more likely to engage with people they already follow. As Bier stated, “We noticed this data was missing from the algo and it made your friends appear less in your replies.” This led to a situation where users were more likely to encounter spam, financial scams, and malware in the reply section.

item.title: X Algorithm Fix
item.description:
Introduction to the Issue  

X, a social media platform, has been facing issues with its algorithm, which was “missing” data about surfacing posts from people who you’ve followed back. This led to a “battleground” feel on the site, where users were more likely to engage with random arguments rather than posts from their mutuals. According to Nikita Bier, X’s head of product, the algorithm’s issue needed to be addressed to enhance the sense of community on the platform.

item.title: Instagram Takes On Streaming
item.description:
Introduction to Instagram’s New Venture  

Instagram, a social media platform owned by Meta, is expanding its reach into the world of streaming services. The company has announced plans to introduce longer-form, episodic, and live formats to its TV app, which was launched last year. This move is seen as an attempt to bring Instagram to more TV users and capture a larger share of the streaming market.

item.title: Polymarket Fake Bets Scandal
item.description:
Introduction to the Polymarket Scandal  

The world of online betting and social media has been shaken by a recent investigation by the Wall Street Journal, revealing that Polymarket, a popular online betting platform, has been paying individuals to create fake videos of themselves placing bets and celebrating wins on social media. This scandal has sparked outrage and raised concerns about the authenticity of online content and the potential for manipulation. In this article, we will delve into the details of the scandal, explore the implications, and examine the technical aspects of how these fake videos were created and disseminated.
